dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6983 IH: Preventing Rate Inflation in Consumer Energy Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Menendez (for himself and Mr. Casar) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To require data centers to generate electricity, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Preventing Rate Inflation in Consumer Energy Act or the PRICE Act.

section.header: Requirement for data centers to generate electricity

subsection.text: Each data center located in the United States shall generate all of the electricity the data center consumes each year.

paragraph.text: During the period beginning on January 1, 2035, and ending on the date described in paragraph (2), not less than 75 percent of the electricity generated by a data center for purposes of subsection (a) shall be from a clean energy source.
Beginning on January 1, 2040, 100 percent of the electricity generated by a data center for purposes of subsection (a) shall be from a clean energy source.
Any data center that violates any provision of this section shall be subject to a civil penalty of not more than $100,000 per day until the violation is rectified.
Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Energy shall establish an administrative process for the issuance of civil penalties for violations of this section.
The term clean energy source includes a solar, wind, battery, green hydrogen, hydropower, and geothermal source.
The term data center means a facility that—

subparagraph.text: is described in section 453(a)(1) of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 (42 U.S.C. 17112(a)(1)); and
consumes at least 50 megawatts each day.
